revision-history.revision: Added animation strategy section (performance-optimized, shared motion package)
Added app-shell strategy, token architecture SSOT, updated phases

overview.problem:
The current beings.com WordPress site has accumulated significant technical debt:
1. MAINTENANCE BURDEN: 26 active plugins requiring constant updates 2. ELEMENTOR LOCK-IN: Pages built with proprietary Elementor + Element Pack + UICore 3. PERFORMANCE: PageSpeed ~75/100 despite W3 Total Cache 4. SECURITY SURFACE: Each plugin is a potential vulnerability 5. WORKFLOW FRICTION: Cannot "vibe code" with Cursor - requires WP Admin GUI 6. REBRAND TIMING: Visual rebrand already planned - rebuild vs migrate
For a 3-person team chasing unicorn growth, every hour spent on WordPress maintenance is an hour not spent on the core product.
overview.solution:
Build a new marketing site from scratch using:
- ASTRO: Static site generator (HTML-first, React islands) - TAILWIND: Utility-first CSS (AI-friendly, fast iteration) - CLOUDFLARE PAGES: Edge deployment (same infra as product) - GIT: Single source of truth (content as code) - CURSOR: AI-first development workflow
Share brand DNA with the product (colors, typography, motion) while optimizing for marketing conversion rather than data manipulation.

goals.goal: Single source of truth in Git

non-goals.item: Build a CMS admin interface (content lives in Git)
Support non-technical content editors (team is technical)

principles.principle: Static by Default - No database, no server
Islands for Interactivity - React only where needed
Brand DNA Shared - Same tokens as product
Content as Code - MDX files in Git
AI-First Authoring - Optimized for Cursor workflow
Shell-First Design - Pixel-perfect product replica as foundation

layer.name: Framework
layer.rationale: Static-first, React islands, excellent Cloudflare support

layer.name: Styling
layer.rationale: AI writes it perfectly, rapid iteration, small bundle

layer.name: Interactive Islands
layer.rationale: Same as product, can share components if needed

layer.name: Content
layer.rationale: Markdown + React components for rich content

layer.name: Deployment
layer.rationale: Same infra as product, edge CDN, zero cold starts

layer.name: Analytics
layer.rationale: Privacy-friendly option or keep existing tracking

layer.name: Animation
layer.rationale: ~5KB total vs ~55KB for Framer Motion + GSAP. Meets PageSpeed 95+ target.

architecture.directory-structure: beings-website/ # Repository (beings-marketing) ├── astro.config.mjs # Astro configuration ├── tailwind.config.mjs # Tailwind with brand tokens ├── package.json ├── wrangler.toml # Cloudflare Pages config │ ├── docs/ │ ├── bw-prd-01-rebrand.xml # This PRD (Source of Truth) │ └── screenshots/ # Reference images │ ├── product-reference.png # Full product screenshot │ ├── product-rail.png # Rail detail reference │ └── shell-final.png # Verified shell implementation │ ├── src/ │ ├── assets/ │ │ └── logo.svg # Shared with product │ │ │ ├── styles/ │ │ ├── brand-tokens.css # SSOT: Colors, radii, shadows, motion │ │ ├── app-shell.css # Shell layout tokens (refs brand-tokens) │ │ └── global.css # Base styles (imports above) │ │ │ ├── components/ │ │ ├── app-shell/ # ★ PRODUCT REPLICA COMPONENTS │ │ │ ├── AppShell.astro # Root grid layout (rail + main) │ │ │ ├── SidebarRail.astro # 80px nav rail with icons │ │ │ └── MainPanel.astro # White floating content panel │ │ │ │ │ ├── layout/ │ │ │ ├── Header.astro │ │ │ ├── Footer.astro │ │ │ ├── Layout.astro │ │ │ └── MobileMenu.tsx # React island for mobile nav │ │ │ │ │ ├── marketing/ │ │ │ ├── Hero.astro │ │ │ ├── FeatureGrid.astro │ │ │ ├── Testimonial.astro │ │ │ ├── PricingTable.astro │ │ │ ├── CTASection.astro │ │ │ └── ProductScreenshot.astro │ │ │ │ │ └── islands/ # React components (interactive) │ │ ├── CostCalculator.tsx # Migrated from WP plugin │ │ ├── ContactForm.tsx # HubSpot integration │ │ └── ProductDemo.tsx # Embedded product preview │ │ │ ├── pages/ │ │ ├── index.astro # Homepage │ │ ├── shell-test.astro # Shell verification page │ │ ├── about.astro │ │ ├── pricing.astro │ │ ├── contact.astro │ │ └── blog/ │ │ ├── index.astro # Blog listing │ │ └── [...slug].astro # Dynamic blog posts │ │ │ └── content/ │ ├── config.ts # Astro content collections │ └── blog/ │ ├── 2026-01-23-qualitative-coding.mdx │ └── ... # Migrated from WordPress │ ├── public/ │ ├── favicon.ico │ ├── og-image.png │ └── robots.txt │ └── scripts/ └── migrate-wp-posts.ts # One-time WordPress export

brand-tokens.description: Extract design tokens from the product (beings/src/theme/*) to ensure brand continuity. The marketing site should FEEL like the same brand while being optimized for a different job (conversion vs data manipulation).
brand-tokens.token-architecture:
TOKEN ARCHITECTURE (SSOT Chain)
The marketing site uses a two-file token system to maintain DRY principles while enabling pixel-perfect product replication:
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ PRODUCT (beings/src/theme/*) │ │ palette.ts, m3Tokens.ts, layout-constants.ts │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ ▼ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ brand-tokens.css (SSOT for marketing site) │ │ Colors, radii, shadows, motion, state layers, spacing │ │ Dark mode auto-switches via [data-theme="dark"] │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ ▼ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ app-shell.css (Shell-specific tokens) │ │ References brand-tokens.css for all design values │ │ Defines ONLY shell layout dimensions: │ │ - --shell-rail-width: 5rem (80px) │ │ - --shell-panel-radius: var(--radius-md) │ │ - --shell-panel-border-width: 3.75px │ │ - --shell-icon-button-size: 48px │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ ▼ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Components (AppShell.astro, SidebarRail.astro, etc.) │ │ Use --shell-* tokens for layout, inherit colors/motion │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
RULES: 1. brand-tokens.css is the SSOT - never duplicate values elsewhere 2. app-shell.css REFERENCES brand-tokens, doesn't redefine 3. Components use --shell-* tokens (semantic naming) 4. When product changes, update brand-tokens.css first
Last synced with product: 2026-01-23

visual-philosophy.description:
The marketing site must FEEL like the same product. This section defines the visual DNA that makes Beings look like "a tool" rather than "a website."
Source: beings/src/theme/*, beings/src/layouts/*
visual-philosophy.canvas-and-card:
KEY CONCEPT: "Canvas + Card" Layout
The product uses a distinctive visual pattern: 1. CANVAS: Light gray background (#F0F4F9 "Gemini Gray") provides breathing room 2. CARD: White floating panels (#FFFFFF) contain content with subtle elevation 3. DEPTH: The card "floats" above the canvas, creating visual hierarchy
This creates the "app-like" feel that distinguishes modern SaaS from generic websites.
IMPLEMENTATION: - Page background: var(--color-background) #F0F4F9 - Content panels: var(--color-surface) #FFFFFF with border-radius: 16px - Subtle shadow: elevation level 2 or 3 - DO NOT use full-width colored sections that span edge-to-edge
visual-philosophy.anti-patterns:
AVOID these "generic SaaS" patterns:
1. HEAVY GRADIENTS: No purple-to-blue gradients spanning full sections → Instead: Solid colors or very subtle gradients within cards
2. BLOCKY SECTIONS: No full-width colored bands alternating down the page → Instead: Content in floating cards on gray canvas
3. OVERSIZED RADII: No rounded-3xl or rounded-full on large elements → Instead: Use M3 radius scale (8/12/16/28px)
4. THICK BORDERS: No 2px+ borders on cards → Instead: 1px var(--color-divider) or elevation shadow only
5. TEXT TRANSFORM: No UPPERCASE button text → Instead: textTransform: 'none' (sentence case)
6. BLURRY GLASS: No backdrop-filter blur effects for headers → Instead: Solid white with subtle shadow
visual-philosophy.header-style:
HEADER AESTHETIC:
The product header (AppBar) is: - Transparent background on canvas - Solid white when scrolled (no blur) - Subtle shadow on scroll: elevation level 2 - Clean, minimal - not a design statement
For marketing, adapt as: - Sticky position - bg-surface (white) with elevation-2 shadow - OR transparent over hero, solid white after scroll - NO backdrop-blur - it looks messy with our palette
visual-philosophy.button-style:
BUTTON AESTHETIC (from beings/src/theme/components.ts):
- Border radius: 8px (small) - Text transform: none (not uppercase) - Font weight: 600 (semibold, not bold) - Press effect: scale(0.98) on :active - Transitions: 150ms ease-in-out
Primary button: - Background: var(--color-primary) - Text: white - Hover: var(--color-primary-light)
Secondary/Outlined button: - Background: transparent - Border: 1px solid var(--color-divider) - Text: var(--color-text-primary) - Hover: background with state-layer opacity 0.08

animation-strategy.description:
Performance-optimized animation strategy for the Astro 4.x architecture. Designed to meet PageSpeed 95+ target while enabling rich product demos.
CONSTRAINT: Total animation library payload must stay under 10KB gzipped.

tool-allocation.layer:
Standard CSS transitions for hover states, focus rings, button presses. Uses M3 motion tokens from brand-tokens.css.
Example: Button hover, card hover, link underlines Payload: 0KB (native CSS)

Native Astro feature for navigation between pages. No additional libraries required.
Example: Home → Blog, Pricing → Contact Payload: 0KB (browser API) Browser support: ~75% (graceful fallback to normal navigation)

Lightweight alternative to Framer Motion for complex staggers. Used inside React islands for panel entrances, chip animations.
Package: motion (npm install motion) Alternative: @motionone/react for React-specific bindings
Example: Staggered panel entrance, Aida chips appearing Payload: ~4KB gzipped

Replaces GSAP for typing/streaming effects. Custom useTypewriter React hook using requestAnimationFrame.
Location: src/hooks/useTypewriter.ts
Example: Aida chat message appearing character-by-character Payload: ~0.5KB

animation-strategy.island-architecture:
INTERACTIVE ISLAND PATTERN The animated App Shell demo is a self-contained React island. Animation libraries only load when the user scrolls to the demo. ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ src/pages/index.astro (Static HTML - 0KB JS) │ │ │ │
│ │

Static marketing content

← No JS, SEO-friendly │ │
│ │ │ │
│ │ ← Hydrates on scroll │ │
│ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ HYDRATION DIRECTIVES: - client:visible — Loads JS when element enters viewport (recommended) - client:load — Loads JS immediately on page load (for above-fold) - client:idle — Loads JS when browser is idle The demo island contains: - React state (isSidebarOpen, messages, etc.) - Motion One for panel entrances - useTypewriter for chat streaming - All state is SELF-CONTAINED (no Astro ↔ React state passing)
animation-strategy.shared-motion-package:
SHARED ANIMATION ASSETS (DRY Principle)
Motion tokens and utilities can be shared between the marketing site and the main product to ensure consistent animation feel.
SHARING LEVELS: ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Level 1: CSS Variables (tokens.css) — Low coupling │ │ Level 2: CSS Keyframes (keyframes.css) — Low coupling │ │ Level 3: Animation Configs (variants.ts) — Medium coupling │ │ Level 4: Hooks (useTypewriter.ts) — Medium coupling │ │ Level 5: Full Components — High coupling │ └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
RECOMMENDED STRUCTURE (if shared package created):
packages/@beings/motion/ ├── tokens.css # CSS variables (easings, durations) ├── keyframes.css # Reusable @keyframes definitions ├── variants.ts # Framework-agnostic animation configs ├── hooks/ │ ├── useTypewriter.ts │ └── useStaggerChildren.ts └── index.ts
INITIAL APPROACH: Start by sharing tokens.css and keyframes.css (copy between repos). Graduate to npm package if sharing proves valuable.
DO NOT SHARE: - MUI-specific transition components (product only) - Tailwind utility classes (different styling systems) - Full animated React components (different component libraries)
animation-strategy.m3-motion-tokens:
M3 MOTION TOKENS (Must match product)
These values ensure animations FEEL the same as the product, regardless of which animation library implements them.
EASINGS (from beings/src/theme/m3Tokens.ts): --ease-standard: cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1) /* Most transitions */ --ease-emphasized: cubic-bezier(0.2, 0, 0, 1) /* Attention-grabbing */ --ease-decelerate: cubic-bezier(0.05, 0.7, 0.1, 1) /* Enter animations */
DURATIONS: --duration-fast: 150ms /* Micro-interactions (button press) */ --duration-medium: 300ms /* Standard transitions (panel slide) */ --duration-long: 500ms /* Complex/large movements */
MUI COMPATIBILITY: MUI uses react-transition-group + CSS internally. It doesn't have a proprietary animation engine. As long as we use the same easing curves and durations, animations will look identical whether implemented with CSS, Motion One, or Framer Motion.
animation-strategy.implementation-checklist:
SETUP: 1. [ ] npm install motion (Motion One) — DO NOT install framer-motion or gsap 2. [ ] Create src/hooks/useTypewriter.ts 3. [ ] Add motion tokens to brand-tokens.css (if not present) 4. [ ] Configure Tailwind with motion-related utilities
COMPONENTS: 5. [ ] Create src/components/islands/AppShellDemo.tsx 6. [ ] Implement sidebar collapse with CSS transitions 7. [ ] Implement panel entrances with Motion One 8. [ ] Implement chat typing with useTypewriter hook
VERIFICATION: 9. [ ] Lighthouse audit: Animation JS < 10KB 10. [ ] Visual comparison: Matches product motion feel 11. [ ] Mobile test: Animations don't cause jank
animation-strategy.anti-patterns:
AVOID:
1. Framer Motion for simple transitions → Use CSS transitions instead (0KB)
2. GSAP for typing effects → Use vanilla JS with requestAnimationFrame (~0.5KB)
3. Animation libraries in non-island components → Keep marketing pages JS-free where possible
4. Passing animation state between Astro and React → Keep island state self-contained
5. Spring physics libraries (react-spring, etc.) → M3 uses CSS bezier curves, not spring physics

app-shell-strategy.description:
The marketing site is built "shell-first" - starting with a pixel-perfect replica of the product's app shell, then layering marketing content inside.
This approach enables a future "seamless transition" where the real product can be lazy-loaded in the background, allowing instant app access.
app-shell-strategy.rationale:
WHY SHELL-FIRST?
1. BRAND CONTINUITY: The shell IS the product's visual identity 2. LAZY-LOAD VISION: Background-download real app for seamless transition 3. SINGLE SOURCE: Same CSS tokens = automatic visual alignment 4. TRUST SIGNAL: Marketing that looks like the product builds confidence
The goal is for visitors to feel they're already "inside the product" before they've even signed up.
app-shell-strategy.shell-anatomy:
PRODUCT SHELL ANATOMY (from beings/src/layouts/*)
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ .app-shell (CSS Grid) │ │ display: grid │ │ grid-template-columns: 80px 1fr │ │ height: 100dvh │ │ background: var(--shell-canvas) #F0F4F9 │ │ │ │ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │.sidebar- │ │ .shell-main │ │ │ │ rail │ │ padding: 8px (creates gray gap around panel) │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ 80px │ │ ┌────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ │ sticky │ │ │ .main-panel │ │ │ │ │ 100dvh │ │ │ background: white │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ border-radius: 12px (uniform) │ │ │ │ │ hamburger│ │ │ border: 3.75px solid canvas │ │ │ │ │ workspaces│ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ ──────── │ │ │ [MARKETING CONTENT GOES HERE] │ │ │ │ │ trash │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ settings │ │ └────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ │ └──────────┘ └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
KEY DIMENSIONS (from DevTools inspection): - Rail width: 80px (5rem) - Panel radius: 12px (uniform all corners) - Panel border: 3.75px solid canvas (creates floating effect) - Content padding: 8px (MUI theme.spacing(1)) - Icon button size: 48px - Icon size: 24px - Rail border: 0.625px solid divider (right edge)
KEY BEHAVIORS: - Sidebar sticky at 100dvh (viewport locked) - Main panel fills remaining space - App shell height constrained to 100dvh (no overflow)

shell-components.component:
Root layout wrapper with CSS Grid. - grid-template-columns: var(--shell-rail-width) 1fr - height: 100dvh (viewport locked) - overflow: hidden (no scrollbars on shell itself)
Contains: SidebarRail + shell-main wrapper + MainPanel

80px fixed navigation rail (collapsed state).
Structure: - rail-content: hamburger + workspace icons (flex-1) - rail-footer: trash + settings (border-top separator)
Icons: Filled MUI-style SVGs (not stroked) States: hover (8% overlay), active (10% primary tint + blue icon)

White floating content panel.
- background: var(--shell-surface) white - border-radius: 12px (uniform) - border: 3.75px solid var(--shell-canvas) - overflow: hidden (panel-content scrolls)
Contains <slot /> for marketing content.

app-shell-strategy.lazy-load-vision:
FUTURE: Seamless App Transition
The pixel-perfect shell enables a "lazy-load" pattern:
1. Visitor lands on marketing site (static Astro) 2. Background: Service worker preloads real product bundle 3. User clicks "Try It" → shell "morphs" into real app 4. No page reload, instant transition
REQUIREMENTS FOR THIS TO WORK: - Marketing shell must be PIXEL-PERFECT match - Same CSS tokens (already achieved via SSOT) - Same HTML structure for shell elements - Hydration replaces static components with React
This is why we cannot simplify the shell - the precision IS the feature.

component-inventory.description:
Marketing components needed for the rebrand. These are NOT ports of Elementor widgets - they are purpose-built for conversion.
Components are organized by category: - App Shell: Pixel-perfect product replica (foundation) - Layout: Page-level wrappers - Marketing: Content blocks for conversion - Interactive: React islands for dynamic features

category.name: App Shell
category.component:
Root layout wrapper for shell-first pages.
IMPLEMENTS: CSS Grid layout matching product FlatMainLayout.tsx - Gray canvas background (100dvh viewport) - Sidebar rail (80px) + main content area - Content area has 8px padding for floating panel effect
USAGE: <AppShell> <!-- Marketing content here --> </AppShell>
Props: - hideRail: boolean (hide sidebar for certain pages) - class: string (additional styling)

80px vertical navigation rail (collapsed state).
IMPLEMENTS: Product NavigationRailHeader.tsx + Sidebar.tsx
Structure: - Header: Hamburger button (no logo) - Nav: Personal workspace (active), Org workspaces (disabled) - Footer: Trash + Settings (with border-top separator)
Icons: Filled MUI-style SVGs - Hamburger: text-primary color - Active: primary color (10% tint bg) - Disabled: 60% opacity, action.disabled color
KEY DETAILS: - height: 100dvh (sticky, viewport locked) - align-items: flex-start (left-aligned, not centered) - padding: 12px top/bottom, 16px left - border-right: 0.625px solid divider

White floating content panel inside the shell.
IMPLEMENTS: Product panel aesthetics from Dockview groups
Styling: - background: white - border-radius: 12px (uniform all corners) - border: 3.75px solid canvas-color (floating effect) - No shadow (product doesn't use shadow on main panel)
Contains <slot /> for marketing content. Panel-content area scrolls independently.

category.name: Layout
category.component:
Navigation bar with logo, links, CTA button.
VISUAL SPEC (from product AppBar): - Sticky position with z-index above content - Background: var(--color-surface) solid white (NO blur) - Shadow on scroll: elevation-2 (subtle) - Height: 64px desktop, 56px mobile - Content: max-width container, centered
ELEMENTS: - Logo (left): links to home - Nav links (center/right): Product, Solutions, Pricing, About - CTA button (right): "Get Started" - primary style, radius-small (8px) - Mobile: hamburger menu → React island for state
DO NOT: - Use backdrop-blur (looks messy) - Use gradient backgrounds - Make the header a design statement (keep it clean)
Logo, navigation links, social icons, legal links, newsletter signup. Background: var(--color-background) gray canvas, not a colored band.
Base layout wrapper with Header, Footer, SEO meta tags. Page background: var(--color-background) #F0F4F9 (Gemini Gray).

category.name: Hero Sections
category.component:
Homepage hero implementing "Canvas + Card" aesthetic.
VISUAL SPEC: - Background: var(--color-background) gray canvas - Content: centered in a floating white card OR text directly on canvas - NO heavy gradients - if gradient needed, keep it very subtle
LAYOUT OPTION A - "Floating Panel": - Gray canvas background - White card (radius-large 16px, elevation-3) contains hero content - Creates depth and matches product workspace feel
LAYOUT OPTION B - "Clean Canvas": - Gray canvas background - Text directly on canvas (no card) - Product screenshot in a white card below
ELEMENTS: - H1: Large headline with optional primary-colored word - Subtitle: var(--color-text-secondary), max 2 lines - Dual CTAs: Primary button + Secondary/outlined button - Product visual: Screenshot in elevated card OR video
DO NOT: - Use purple-to-blue gradient backgrounds - Make buttons rounded-full (use radius-small 8px) - Add excessive decoration or flourishes
Interior page hero: H1, breadcrumbs, optional subtitle. Background: var(--color-background) gray canvas. Content: centered, no card wrapper needed.

category.name: Content Blocks
category.component:
3-column grid of feature cards.
VISUAL SPEC: - Container: on gray canvas OR inside a white section card - Cards: var(--color-surface) white, radius-medium (12px) - Border: 1px var(--color-divider) OR elevation-1 shadow (pick one) - Icons: 24px, var(--color-primary) or var(--color-text-secondary)
CARD CONTENT: - Icon (top): simple, not elaborate - Title: h4 size, font-weight-600 - Description: body text, var(--color-text-secondary)
SPACING: - Card padding: 24px (p-6) - Grid gap: 24px (gap-6)
DO NOT: - Use emoji as icons (use Lucide/Heroicons) - Add hover transforms or heavy shadows - Make cards too tall (keep content concise)
Alternating image/text sections for detailed feature explanation.
Customer quote with avatar, name, company, optional logo.
Swipeable testimonial slider for mobile.
"Trusted by" section with customer/partner logos.
Key metrics display (e.g., "10,000+ researchers").

category.name: Conversion
category.component:
Pricing tiers with feature comparison, CTA buttons.
VISUAL SPEC: - Container: on gray canvas - Cards: white surface, radius-medium (12px), elevation-2 - Recommended tier: subtle primary border or background tint - Toggle: radius-full (pill shape) for monthly/annual switch
DO NOT: - Use heavy shadows or gradients on cards - Make one card dramatically larger than others

Call-to-action section for page bottoms.
VISUAL SPEC (CRITICAL - avoid heavy gradients):
OPTION A - "Card CTA": - Gray canvas background (continues from page) - White card with radius-large (16px), elevation-2 - Centered headline + button inside card
OPTION B - "Primary CTA": - Solid var(--color-primary) background (no gradient) - White text, white outlined button - Keep it clean and confident
DO NOT: - Use purple-to-blue gradient (looks dated) - Make the section too tall (compact is better) - Add decorative elements or patterns
ELEMENTS: - Headline: H2 or H3 size - Optional subtext: 1 line max - Single CTA button: prominent, radius-small (8px)

React component with HubSpot API integration.
VISUAL SPEC: - Container: white card on gray canvas, radius-medium (12px) - Inputs: radius-small (8px), 1px border var(--color-divider) - Focus state: primary color border, subtle shadow - Button: primary style, radius-small (8px)
Form validation, loading states, success message.

category.name: Interactive (React Islands)
category.component: Migrate from WordPress plugin. React component with pricing inputs, calculation logic, result display.
Embedded preview of product UI. Could be iframe or simplified React component.
Lazy-loaded video with custom controls, Vimeo/YouTube embed.

category.name: Blog
category.component: Post preview with featured image, title, excerpt, date.
Full article layout with MDX rendering, table of contents, author info, related posts.
Author bio with avatar, social links.

content-migration.description: One-time migration of blog content from WordPress to MDX files. Pages will be redesigned from scratch (not migrated).

blog-posts.strategy:
1. Export posts via WordPress REST API (already connected via MCP) 2. Convert HTML content to Markdown using turndown 3. Extract metadata (title, date, author, categories) to frontmatter 4. Download featured images to local assets 5. Manual review for formatting issues
Expected: ~50 blog posts, mostly clean HTML (not Elementor).
blog-posts.frontmatter-schema: --- title: "Types of Qualitative Coding in Research Methodology" description: "Learn about the different coding approaches..." pubDate: 2026-01-23 updatedDate: 2026-01-23 author: "beings-team" category: "methodology" tags: ["qualitative", "coding", "research"] image: "./images/qualitative-coding-hero.webp" ---

content-migration.pages:
Pages will NOT be migrated - they will be redesigned from scratch. The Elementor layouts are not portable and the rebrand requires new visual design anyway.
Key pages to build: - Homepage (complete redesign) - About (new design) - Pricing (new design with PricingTable component) - Contact (new design with HubSpot form) - Product pages (CloudLab, Aida - new designs)
content-migration.assets: - Logo: Copy from beings/src/assets/logo.svg - Product screenshots: Take fresh screenshots from product - Stock images: Evaluate and replace with on-brand imagery - Icons: Use Heroicons or Lucide (consistent with Tailwind ecosystem)

seo-strategy.description: Replace RankMath Pro functionality with static-first SEO.

meta-tags.implementation:
Each page/post defines SEO in frontmatter:
--- title: "Page Title" description: "Meta description under 160 chars" canonical: "https://beings.com/page" ogImage: "./og-image.png" ---
Layout.astro uses @astrojs/seo or custom <SEO /> component to render all meta tags at build time.

sitemap.note: Auto-generated at build time, submitted to Search Console

redirects.note: Map old WordPress URLs to new structure

integration.name: HubSpot
integration.new: 1. Tracking: Paste HubSpot tracking code in Layout.astro <head> 2. Forms: Either embed HubSpot forms (iframe) or build custom React forms that POST to HubSpot API 3. Chat: HubSpot chat widget via script tag

integration.name: Analytics
integration.new:
Option A: Keep GTM - paste container ID in Layout.astro Option B: Switch to Plausible for privacy-friendly analytics
Recommendation: Keep GTM for now to maintain marketing continuity.

integration.name: Cookie Consent
integration.new: Paste CookieYes script tag in Layout.astro. Same functionality, no plugin.

integration.name: Cost Calculator
integration.new:
Rebuild as React component (island).
Steps: 1. Document current calculator fields and formulas 2. Build CostCalculator.tsx with same logic 3. Style with Tailwind to match brand 4. Embed on pricing page with client:visible

phase.name: App Shell Foundation
phase.description: Build pixel-perfect replica of product shell as the visual foundation. This is the "frame" that all marketing content will live inside.
phase.success-criteria: Shell visually indistinguishable from product when compared side-by-side. Verified via DevTools inspection of: - Rail width (80px), icon sizes (48px/24px), colors - Panel radius (12px), border width (3.75px) - Footer alignment, icon states, spacing

phase.name: Panel Components
phase.description: Build interior panel components that will contain marketing content. These live INSIDE the MainPanel slot.

deliverables.item: Header with navigation (inside panel, not full-width)

phase.success-criteria: Can assemble a complete homepage inside the shell.

phase.name: Key Pages
phase.description: Build core marketing pages using the shell + panel components. All pages use AppShell wrapper for consistent product feel.
phase.success-criteria: Core marketing funnel complete. Pages feel like "the product's marketing face."

phase.name: Blog Migration

deliverables.item: All blog posts migrated to MDX

phase.success-criteria: All blog URLs working with redirects from old structure.

phase.name: Integrations
phase.success-criteria: Marketing team confirms all tracking operational.

phase.name: Launch + Lazy-Load Prep
phase.description: Final launch preparation and groundwork for future lazy-load transition.

deliverables.item: Redirects file for all old URLs
Service worker stub for future app preload

phase.success-criteria: beings.com serving new Astro site with no broken links. Shell verified against product for lazy-load compatibility.

success-metrics.metric: Google PageSpeed Insights score on mobile.
Time spent on plugin updates, security patches.
Time from Git push to live on Cloudflare.

risk.mitigation: Comprehensive redirects file. Keep URL structure where possible. Submit new sitemap to Search Console immediately.

risk.description: Cost Calculator logic errors in rebuild
risk.mitigation: Document exact formulas from existing plugin before rebuild. Side-by-side testing with WordPress version.
Test all form submissions and tracking events in staging. Marketing team sign-off before launch.
Manual review of top 20 posts. Automated checks for broken images.

question.question: Should beings-marketing be a separate repo or live in the beings monorepo?
question.recommendation: Separate repo recommended. Different deployment pipeline, different concerns. Can share brand tokens via npm package or copy.

question.question: Will anyone other than the 3-person technical team edit content?
question.recommendation: If yes, add Keystatic or TinaCMS later. For now, MDX in Git is sufficient for a technical team.

question.question: How many product-specific pages (CloudLab, Aida) need to be built?
question.recommendation: Audit current WordPress pages. May be able to consolidate to fewer, more impactful pages.

appendix.wordpress-decommission-checklist:
After successful launch:
1. [ ] Export full database backup 2. [ ] Export all media files (/wp-content/uploads/) 3. [ ] Document any remaining integrations 4. [ ] Cancel plugin licenses (Elementor Pro, Element Pack, etc.) 5. [ ] Set WordPress to maintenance mode 6. [ ] Keep hosting active for 30 days (rollback safety) 7. [ ] After 30 days: Cancel hosting, archive backups
appendix.cursor-prompts:
Useful prompts for building with Cursor:
FOUNDATION: "Create a new Astro project with Tailwind CSS, TypeScript, and @astrojs/react. Configure for Cloudflare Pages deployment. Set up the brand tokens from bw-prd-01-rebrand.xml."
COMPONENTS: "Build a Hero component for the marketing site. Use the brand colors from brand-tokens.css. Include: H1, subtitle, two CTA buttons (primary and secondary), and a product screenshot area. Dark mode support required."
MIGRATION: "Write a script that fetches all posts from the WordPress REST API at beings.com, converts the HTML content to Markdown, and saves each post as an MDX file with proper frontmatter."
